title: Latex beads behavior assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c3ubj","text":"Protocol for testing latex beads agreggation after resuspension in different buffers. It allows determining how beads storage conditions can influence their ability to move freely trhough a membrane.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7cs13","text":"In the following assay alittle ammount of beads suspension wil be eluted trhough a membrane. steps: Centrifugate 4 different aliquots of 40 μL 2.5 %wt. latex bead stock suspension at 15.000 rpm for 3 minutes. After centrifugation remove the supernatat slowly.

Resuspend the beads in 100 μL of the four different buffers: distilled water, PBS-T, Bicarbonate Buffer and MES buffer. For resuspending beads repeated pipetting it's highly recommended, aspiring and blowing out in the eppendorf tube. Label the different eppendorfs with the buffer added.
For beads migration test, wait 10 minutes and pipette 10μL of each bead suspension on a piece of nitrocellulose membrane. Separate each drop one to each other at least 8 mm.

Wait until the deposited sample has migrated completely, and circle on the plastic backing of the nitrocellulose membrane the outline of the sample eluted front. Vissible results of beads retention in the membrane should be vissible at this point.
Repeat step 1 and 2, resuspending all the beads in distilled water.
Repeat step 3-4 with the resuspended bead stocks.
creator: Jorge Fernandez Mendez
